title: Augmented Human Development Index
subtitle: The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.
caption:
Augmented Human Development Index

The Augmented Human Development Index (AHDI) is a summary measure of achievement in four key dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, being knowledgeable, being free and having a decent standard of living.

This choropleth map displays the Augmented Human Development Index for the year 2020, utilizing a color scale ranging from 0 to 0.8 to represent index values across different nations. The darkest blue shades, indicating the highest index scores between 0.7 and 0.8, are visible in countries such as Australia, Canada, the United States, Norway, Sweden, Finland, and Japan. A slightly lighter band of blue, corresponding to values roughly between 0.5 and 0.7, covers much of Western and Southern Europe, including the United Kingdom, France, Germany, and Spain, as well as nations in South America like Chile and Argentina. Mid-range values, represented by medium blue tones approximately between 0.3 and 0.5, appear in regions including Mexico, Brazil, Russia, China, and parts of Eastern Europe. Lighter shades of blue and purple, signifying lower index scores between 0.1 and 0.3, dominate the visual landscape of Africa and parts of South Asia, including India. The palest colors on the scale, representing values near 0 to 0.1, are found in specific areas of Central Africa and Afghanistan. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching to indicate that no data is available for that territory. The gradient transitions smoothly from the lightest hues in the lowest-performing regions to the deepest saturation in the highest-performing regions, providing a global overview of the distribution of these development metrics.
